untranslatable_reason: Double Bottom pattern requires identifying two distinct bottoms at similar price levels and the highest high between them. This multi-bar structural pattern detection (local minima identification and confirmation point tracking across multiple bars) cannot be expressed using single-bar indicator conditions with the allowed operators and indicators. The pattern requires state tracking across 5-20+ bars to identify the two bottoms and measure the recovery between them, which is beyond the scope of point-in-time indicator comparisons.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: Used Donchian Channel upper crossover as a simplified breakout signal. However, true Double Bottom pattern recognition requires: (1) Historical bar analysis to find two local minima, (2) Verification they are at similar levels, (3) Confirmation of recovery between them, (4) Tracking the highest high in the middle section. These require pattern state machine logic, not available in allowed operators. The 78% success rate and 40% average rise suggest this is a high-confidence pattern, but cannot be accurately translated to simple indicator rules.
